title: Đề luyện tập 06 - RISC-V, Cache và Pipeline
description: Đề luyện tập 06. Mỗi câu 10 điểm, tổng 100 điểm.

questions.title: 5-stage pipeline with forwarding and hazard detection
questions.prompt:
Assume that the following code sequence is executed on the 5-stage pipelined processor with forwarding and hazard detection units shown below:
add x5,  x1, x2
lw  x6,  0(x5)
add x7,  x6, x8
sub x9,  x7, x5
sw  x9,  4(x5)
For the first seven cycles, specify the values of control signals at A, B, C, D, E, F, and G.
questions.solution: This sequence includes a load-use hazard: lw x6 is immediately followed by add x7, x6, x8. In cycle 4, PCWrite and IF/IDWrite are disabled and the control mux inserts a bubble, so A=0, B=0, E=0. After the bubble, x6 is forwarded from MEM/WB to the add instruction in cycle 6, and x7 is forwarded from EX/MEM to the sub instruction in cycle 7. This is intentionally different from the earlier add → lw → lw → or → sw pattern.

questions.title: Cache parameters: 2-way set associative, write-back
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
■ 2-Way Set Associative Cache
■ Write policy: Write-back using write allocate with one Dirty bit per block
■ Block size is 64 bytes
■ Cache size is 64 KB
■ Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 64KB/64B = 1024. Sets = 1024/2 = 512. Index = 9, offset = 6, tag = 17. Total = 1024×(512+17+valid 1+dirty 1) = 543744 bits.

questions.title: Fully associative cache với true LRU
questions.prompt:
Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a fully associative cache with 2-word blocks and a total size of 8 words. Assume one word is 32 bits.

Sequence of word addresses:
0x0a, 0x0b, 0x18, 0x28, 0x0a, 0x2c, 0x19, 0x29, 0x38, 0x0b, 0x2d, 0x39, 0x48, 0x0a

Assume a true LRU replacement policy. For each reference, identify the binary word address, the tag, and hit/miss.

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Offset = 1 bit(s), tag = upper 7 bits. Hit/Miss sequence: M, H, M, M, H, M, H, H, M, M, M, H, M, H. Cache full: yes. Replacements: 4.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: lw
questions.prompt:
Consider a single-cycle RISC-V processor with add, sub, and, or, lw, sw and beq.

Let the processor execute:
lw x22, 128(x13)
at address 6000. Suppose register xi = 36i for i=1,...,31. Suppose memory[M] = M + 750.
questions.solution: For lw, ALU computes x13+128 = 468+128 = 596. Memory[596] = 596+750 = 1346, so x22 receives 1346. D is imm[4:0] = 00000, and L is the value read from that register field.

questions.title: 3-way set associative cache, điền Way theo từng dòng
questions.prompt:
Assume the memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a 3-way set associative cache with 2-word blocks and a total size of 48 words. Assume 1 word = 64 bits.

First, fill in the cache parameters. Then use true LRU. For each reference, identify binary address, tag, set index, offset, hit/miss, and the tags in each way after the reference has been handled.

Lưu ý: Nếu một Way có nhiều dòng như T(001)=0000, T(010)=1011, mỗi dòng sẽ là một ô nhập riêng. Các dòng trong Way giữ theo thứ tự block được đưa vào way đó, không sort theo index.

Sequence of word addresses:
0x12, 0x94, 0x3d, 0x13, 0x9e, 0x6a, 0x9f, 0x2e, 0x4f, 0x95, 0x9f, 0xba, 0x3f, 0xfe

fields_intro.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

fields_extra.label: After these accesses, is the cache full?

questions.solution: Block offset = 1 bit(s), set index = 3 bit(s), tag = 4 bit(s). Hit/Miss sequence: M, M, M, H, M, M, H, M, M, H, H, M, M, M. Cache full: no. Replacements: 2. Way answers keep physical insertion order and are not sorted by set index.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: and
questions.prompt:
Consider a single-cycle RISC-V processor with add, sub, and, or, lw, sw and beq.

Let the processor execute:
and x18, x11, x14
at address 6004. Suppose register xi = 36i for i=1,...,31. Suppose memory[M] = M + 600.
questions.solution: For and, source values are x11=396 and x14=504. ALU result is 392, and this result is written to x18.

questions.title: Fully associative cache parameters
questions.prompt: Assume the main memory is word addressing with 8-bit addresses and the cache is a fully associative cache with 4-word blocks and a total size of 16 words. Assume one word is 32 bits.

fields.label: Number of blocks in the cache
Number of sets in the cache
Number of blocks in the memory
Number of tag bits in each block
Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Block offset = log2(4) = 2. Fully associative means one set and no index. Tag = 8 - 2 = 6. Total bits = 4×(128+6+valid 1) = 540 bits.

questions.title: Single-cycle RISC-V datapath: sw
questions.prompt:
Consider a single-cycle RISC-V processor with add, sub, and, or, lw, sw and beq.

Let the processor execute:
sw x19, -96(x15)
at address 6008. Suppose register xi = 36i for i=1,...,31. Suppose memory[M] = M + 750.
questions.solution: For sw, ALU computes x15+-96 = 540+-96 = 444. The data written to memory is x19 = 684. No register is written back.

questions.title: Execution time: single-cycle vs pipeline
questions.prompt:
Consider the following sequence of 32-bit RISC-V instructions:
add x12, x4, x5
lw  x13, 0(x12)
add x14, x13, x6
or  x15, x14, x7
sw  x15, 4(x12)
sub x16, x12, x8
a) Single-cycle processor clock cycle time = 1000 ps.
b) 5-stage pipelined processor clock cycle time = 250 ps, without forwarding or hazard detection units. Insert NOPs to ensure correct execution.
c) 5-stage pipelined processor clock cycle time = 250 ps, with forwarding/hazard detection units.
d) Which case gives the medium performance?

fields.label: a) Execution time on single-cycle processor
b) Number of cycles after inserting NOPs
b) Execution time without forwarding/hazard detection
c) Number of stalls/NOPs needed with forwarding/hazard detection
c) Execution time with forwarding/hazard detection

questions.solution: Single-cycle: 6×1000 = 6000 ps. Without forwarding/hazard detection, a valid schedule inserts 8 NOPs, so 14 instructions require 14+4=18 cycles => 4500 ps. With forwarding/hazard detection, only the load-use hazard lw x13 -> add x14 needs one stall, so 6+4+1=11 cycles => 2750 ps. Medium performance is case b.

questions.title: Cache parameters: 8-way set associative, write-back
questions.prompt:
Here is the cache with the following key characteristics:
■ 8-Way Set Associative Cache
■ Write policy: Write-back using write allocate with one Dirty bit per block
■ Block size is 32 bytes
■ Cache size is 128 KB
■ Memory is byte addressing with 32-bit addresses

fields.label: Total bits required to implement the cache

questions.solution: Blocks = 128KB/32B = 4096. Sets = 4096/8 = 512. Index = 9, offset = 5, tag = 18. Total = 4096×(256+18+valid 1+dirty 1) = 1130496 bits.
